name: Submit — user can fill form and reach proof-generation step

planSteps.description: If the onboarding modal appears, dismiss it by pressing Escape or clicking the × button in the top-right corner of the modal
Select a metric from the metric selector — pick 'Latency' or the first available option
Enter the value 7 in the baseline severity/score input
Enter the value 3 in the outcome severity/score input
Set the minimum improvement threshold to 20 (either by typing 20 or dragging the slider)
Click the 'Generate Proof' button (or the primary CTA on the form)

After clicking Generate Proof, the page shows a progress indicator, status message, or state change indicating proof generation has started — such as 'Generating proof...', 'Executing circuit...', a loading spinner, or a disabled button state. The page must NOT crash, show a blank screen, or throw a visible JavaScript error.
Wait up to 60 seconds for proof-generation activity to progress (browser WASM proof generation is CPU-heavy and can take 30-60 seconds)

After waiting, the page shows either: (a) a successful proof result with a status indicator, or (b) a clear error message explaining what went wrong (e.g. wallet not connected, network error). It must NOT be stuck in a silent loading state with no user feedback, and must NOT show raw JavaScript stack traces.
